name: Graylog Log Management REST API
what_it_does: Open-source log management and SIEM platform with REST API — collecting, indexing, and analyzing logs from any source via GELF protocol or sidecar agents, with self-hosted deployment backed by Elasticsearch/OpenSearch.
use_cases: Querying log data programmatically for security incident investigation agents
Creating alerts and streams via API for automated log-driven workflows
Ingesting structured log data via GELF HTTP or GELF UDP inputs
Building SIEM workflows that query Graylog for threat hunting and correlation
Automating log retention policy and index management via REST API
not_for: Infrastructure metrics collection (use Prometheus or InfluxDB for metrics)
Teams needing managed SaaS without self-hosting capacity (use Datadog Logs)
Real-time streaming analytics at Kafka scale (Graylog is primarily log-focused)
best_when: A team needs self-hosted log management with SIEM capabilities, Elasticsearch-backed full-text search, and REST API for integration with security and observability workflows.
avoid_when: You need managed SaaS logging, real-time metrics, or don't have capacity to run Elasticsearch infrastructure.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth with username/password or session token. API token can be created per user. REST API available at /api/ with Swagger UI. Self-hosted — credentials managed per deployment.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source: completely free; Graylog Small Business from $1,250/year
pricing.paid_tiers: Small Business from $1,250/year; Enterprise custom; Cloud available
pricing.notes: SSPL open source — free to self-host (check SSPL license implications). Commercial licenses for businesses using Graylog as part of commercial offerings. Strong SIEM and compliance features in enterprise. Popular alternative to Splunk.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — self-hosted; limited by server hardware

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers streams, searches, alerts, inputs, and system management. Swagger UI available at /api/api-browser on self-hosted instances. GELF protocol for log ingestion. Error responses include HTTP status codes and JSON messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Log search queries are idempotent reads. Alert and stream creation idempotent for same names. Log ingestion creates new records — not idempotent by design.
known_agent_gotchas: SSPL license — check implications for commercial use cases
REST API endpoint at /api/ — different from web UI endpoint
Session tokens expire — long-running agents need token refresh or API tokens

security_score_components.security_notes: SSPL open source — code auditable. TLS configurable on self-hosted (recommended with reverse proxy). Basic auth and token auth. Role-based access control. Self-hosted gives full log data control. GDPR friendly.
